impressed.en: I'm very impressed by your work.
impressed.cn: 我对你们的工作印象深刻。

tiring.en: When man and woman unite, work is no longer as tiring.

labour.en: I'm unable to do manual labour.

carelessly.cn: 我不小心打落了一个花瓶。

knitting.en: She was busy with her knitting.

bye.en: I must say good-bye to you.

whilst.en: Whilst at university, Xiaowang held the post of class monitor.
whilst.cn: 上大学的时候，小王担任过班长。

alphabetical.en: Could you rearrange this list so it's in alphabetical order?
alphabetical.cn: 你能把这清单按字母顺序排列吗？

confined.en: He is confined to bed now.

suited.cn: 这件红色的洋装适合她。

stressed.en: He stressed the convenient aspects of city life.
stressed.cn: 他强调都市生活的便利方面。

offence.en: She took offence at my word.

coloured.en: He has a habit of looking down on coloured people.
coloured.cn: 他有瞧不起有色人种的习惯。

bay.en: The airport is in Osaka Bay.

knitted.en: The old woman knitted her brows.

practise.en: Do you still practise your religion?

untidy.en: My room is very untidy. I must put it in order.
untidy.cn: 我的房间很乱。我必须把它弄整齐。

lorry.en: In Britain a truck is referred to as a lorry.

worse.en: I don't know what is worse.

favour.en: Could you do me a favour please?

licence.en: She doesn't have a driving licence.

lord.en: The lord held absolute power over his subjects.
lord.cn: 领主对他的臣民有绝对权力。

grey.en: At night all cats are grey.
grey.cn: 猫在黑暗中都是灰色的。

located.cn: 我们的房子位于交通便利的地方。

April.en: School begins on April the tenth.

C.en: Oranges are rich in vitamin C.
C.cn: 香橙含有丰富的维他命 C。

cent.en: Fifty-two per cent of British women prefer chocolate to sex.
cent.cn: 有百分之五十二的英国女性喜欢巧克力胜于性。

colour.en: Here you can see beautiful colour combinations.
colour.cn: 这里你可以看到漂亮的颜色组合。

customs.en: Each country has its own customs.
customs.cn: 不同的国家有不同的习俗。

email.en: I just wanted to check my email.
email.cn: 我只想查一下我的电子邮件。

Friday.en: Are you free on Friday afternoon?

humour.en: He has a great sense of humour.

kilogram.en: Rice is sold by the kilogram.
kilogram.cn: 米以公斤为单位来出售。

metre.en: String is sold by the metre.

mile.en: This river is one mile across.

pence.en: "Six pence per second," Bob reminds her.
pence.cn: "每秒六便士" 鲍勃提醒她。

pint.en: I want a pint of beer.

Saturday.en: Amy worked in the yard last Saturday.
Saturday.cn: 艾美上星期六在院子里工作。

theatre.en: Romeo and Juliet is on at the theatre.
theatre.cn: 罗密欧和朱丽叶正在剧场上演。

Thursday.en: I want this work done by next Thursday.
Thursday.cn: 我下周四前要这份工作。

tyre.en: A nail penetrated the car tyre.

Wednesday.en: She has been absent since last Wednesday.
Wednesday.cn: 从上周三起，她一直缺席。
